seoTitle: Redlands vs Reedy Swamp — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Redlands and Reedy Swamp. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Redlands vs Reedy Swamp: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Redlands and Reedy Swamp.
heading: Redlands vs Reedy Swamp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Redlands, New South Wales and Reedy Swamp, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Reedy Swamp has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Redlands (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Redlands or Reedy Swamp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Redlands has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Reedy Swamp scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Redlands scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Reedy Swamp scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Redlands or Reedy Swamp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Reedy Swamp has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
